records.ety: [L. Januarius, fr. Janus an old Latin deity, the god of the sun and the year, to whom the month of January was sacred; cf. janua a door, Skr. yā to go.]

defs: The first month of the year, containing thirty-one days.
notes: ☞ Before the adoption of New Style, the commencement of the year was usually reckoned from March 25.
